etymology.description: 爫 (hand, claw) + 又 (hand) = representation of a gesture -> intermediate form changes -> final 爰 form representing 'here, there'
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 爫(yuán, "hand, claw") and 又(yòu, "hand") to visually depict a gesture made with hands. This gesture is historically associated with indicating a specific location, leading to the senses 'here' and 'there'.
